text: Chapter 6 teaches the dominant-to-tonic (V to I) cadence, the foundational harmonic resolution in jazz and traditional music, demonstrated here through the movement from Bb to Eb, with instruction to practice this progression chromatically across the fingerboard. The master's characteristic move is the direct analytical engagement with voicings—students must identify which chord tones each note represents and determine which tones are omitted, training both ear and harmonic comprehension simultaneously. The load-bearing terminology introduced includes V (the dominant) and I (the tonic) as the poles of cadential resolution, along with the concept of chord tone analysis as a systematic approach to understanding voicing construction. The chapter establishes that while multiple cadential closings exist, the voicings presented in Exercises 10, 11, and 12 provide a sufficient toolkit for most purposes, while signaling that additional coloristic variations remain to be explored. This progression-centered pedagogy reflects the method's focus on functional harmony and the student's development as both a player and harmonic analyst.
